overall: The plan is directionally aligned with the architecture, but it is not build-ready: several release-gate capabilities are either missing, mis-tiered, or hidden inside oversized catch-all tasks. The largest risks are the gate evaluator being non-gating, judged-lens model infrastructure being absent, static/context input coverage being incomplete, and CLI/MCP work being bundled beyond agent-session scale.

findings.finding: US-012 gate judged lenses require a configured model and no-model degradation, but the plan has no ModelProvider/JudgmentRunner/BYO-key config task. T-003 config slices also omit model/provider configuration, so agents implementing judged lenses have no executable interface to call.
findings.suggestion: Add a gate-tier model-provider abstraction/config task before judged lenses: env/config resolution, availability check, safe prompt/input boundary, no-model skip result, and tests for US-012 AC2.

findings.finding: US-003 AC1-2 are not actually covered. T-029 is HTML introspection, T-030 is React mapping, and T-042 assumes token-measurable design-system checks, but no task ingests --component/source paths, design tokens, Scaffold docs, records present inputs, or turns token contradictions into findings.
findings.suggestion: Add a gate-tier ContextIngestor/Target construction task covering component/dom/screenshot/source/tokens/scaffold-docs inputs, persisted input provenance, and token-contradiction findings.

findings.finding: The default gate policy is release-gate scope in the PRD, but T-054 GateEvaluator is marked committed and depends on T-016 baseline/waiver work. As written, a release built from only G tasks can expose surface gate in T-061/T-071 without the domain evaluator needed to pass FR-RULE-4.
findings.suggestion: Split T-054 into a G task for default gate policy over new measured P0/P1 findings, never judged/gated, and a C task for baseline/waiver awareness depending on T-016.

findings.finding: CLI verbs bundles init/run/next/status/capture/audit/explain/backlog/validate/gate/baseline/verdict/diff/alternatives/trace plus --json into one gate task. That is far beyond one agent session and mixes gate, committed, and should features into a single release blocker.
findings.suggestion: Split CLI work by vertical command groups and tier them to their backing domain tasks: init/status/run/capture/audit/backlog/explain/gate as G, baseline as C, verdict/diff/alternatives as S.

findings.finding: MCP server + all tools + versioned schemas + snapshots is the same over-bundling problem as T-061. It also depends on T-060 CLI even though architecture says CLI and MCP are sibling adapters over core, not MCP over CLI.
findings.suggestion: Split MCP into server bootstrap/schema infra, gate analytical tools, closed-loop tools, and optional tools. Depend on core services/shared composition factories, not the CLI package.

findings.finding: US-022 / FR-SCORE-7 deterministic fix snippets have no implementation task. T-002 does not name SuggestedPatch, and no task computes contrast hex, aria attributes, or touch-target fixes for measured findings.
findings.suggestion: Add a committed task for SuggestedPatch schema plus deterministic patch generators for contrast, aria, and target-size measured findings; assert judged findings never receive patches.

findings.finding: US-032 AC2 requires GitHub Checks/PR annotations, but T-052 only covers SARIF and T-053 is GitHub Issues export for US-060. Checks/annotations are a different integration surface and acceptance path.
findings.suggestion: Add a committed GitHub Checks/PR annotations reporter/exporter task, local-artifact-first, separate from GitHub Issues export.

findings.finding: US-004, US-014, and US-061 are absent from the task tables despite the plan claiming every story maps to at least one task. These are should-tier, but the review target explicitly requires every US-001..071 story to map to a task.
findings.suggestion: Add S-tier tasks for multi-state/dual-theme capture, cognitive walkthrough/conversion lenses, and Linear/Jira export, or explicitly change the traceability claim to exclude Should stories.

findings.finding: Wave 3 is claimed mutually parallel, but T-041, T-042, and T-043 depend on T-046, which appears later in the same wave. Similar intra-wave chains exist in W0, W1, and W2, so the wave-level independence statement is false.
findings.suggestion: Split waves into subwaves or remove the mutual-parallel claim. For W3, run T-046 first, then judged/content/visual lenses, then registry/overlay integration.

findings.finding: The stated critical path includes T-045 → T-050, but T-050 does not depend on T-045. It also omits release-gate branches that converge at T-060, including capture/auth, grounding, accessibility lens, discovery/overlay, KB entries, and the gate evaluator.
findings.suggestion: Replace the critical path with one computed from explicit deps after expanding T-060's "all core+plugins" into concrete gate dependencies.

findings.finding: Wave 2 is advertised as very high parallelism, but T-020 through T-025 all touch capture selection/backends/security behavior, and T-025 spans capture plus export redaction before reporters/exporters exist. These are not independent leaf tasks.
findings.suggestion: Serialize shared capture registry/security work, split redaction into capture-write redaction and reporter/export redaction, and give the export half dependencies on reporter/export tasks.

findings.finding: Seeded-defect fixture apps for React, Vue, Svelte, and HTML across contrast/focus/target/empty-state is multiple fixture projects and many files, not one ≤3-file task.
findings.suggestion: Split fixtures by framework or by defect matrix, with a separate harness task that consumes already-created fixtures.

findings.finding: Reporting architecture and PRD FR-OUT-1 include per-lens documents, implementation backlog, agent plan, and validation report, but T-050 only creates findings.md and findings.json. The agent-executable backlog/reporting surface is therefore under-tasked.
findings.suggestion: Add report-renderer tasks for backlog/agent-plan and validation-report artifacts, with StateStore-mediated writes and byte-stability tests where applicable.

findings.finding: Authoring all core KB entries across heuristics, a11y, forms, nav, states, visual/content, design systems, and agent implementation is a broad content batch likely touching many files and citations. It is not one agent-sized implementation task.
findings.suggestion: Split KB authoring by category, keep T-047 as the loader/schema acceptance task, and add category-specific content tasks with citation/freshness validation.
